rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidation-induced oxyphosphorylation of alkenes and alkynes with water to give β-ketophosphonates via acetate-mediated HAT generation of P-radicals
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of acetate (nBu4NOAc) generates an acetoxyl radical mediator that abstracts H from P(O)–H; carbon radical intermediate is further anodically oxidized to a carbocation

key_intermediates.name: acetoxyl radical (AcO•) from anodic oxidation of acetate
phosphorus-centered radical (P•) from HAT on diarylphosphine oxide
β-phosphoryl carbon radical (C) from P• addition to alkene
β-phosphoryl carbocation (D) from anodic oxidation of carbon radical
β-hydroxyphosphonate (E) from water attack on carbocation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes acetate to AcO•, which abstracts H from Ar2P(O)H to give a P-centered radical; this P• adds to the alkene to form a β-phosphoryl C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation, trapped by water, and oxidized to the ketone (β-ketophosphonate), with H2 evolved at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Not P–H cleavage (KIE = 1.26); rate-limiting step likely occurs after HAT (e.g., radical addition or subsequent oxidation)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed; product not observed, indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: BHT-P adduct 1e isolated in 61% yield, confirming P-radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2e detected by HRMS; reaction suppressed, supporting radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: deuterated P(O)–H substrate 1b-d
probe_reagents.observation: KH/KD = 1.26 (parallel experiments); P–H cleavage not rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into β-ketophosphonate (2c'), confirming water as oxygen source (86% yield)

probe_reagents.name: isolated β-hydroxyphosphonate (D/E intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: Subjected to standard conditions gave 2c in 75%, supporting path a (hydroxyl intermediate oxidized to ketone)

probe_reagents.name: β-acetoxyphosphonate (path b intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard conditions + DTPB, no 2c formed; rules out path b via acetate adduct

probe_reagents.name: nBu4NOAc / 1b cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: 1b not directly oxidized at anode; nBu4NOAc oxidizes more easily (Ep=2.35 V); new peaks upon mixing indicate electron transfer between them

notes: Two pathways considered after carbocation D: (path a, favored) water nucleophilic attack to give β-hydroxyphosphonate E, then anodic oxidation to β-ketophosphonate; (path b, ruled out) acetate trapping of D to give β-acetoxyphosphonate F. Path b excluded by independent synthesis showing the acetoxy intermediate does not convert to product. 18O labeling confirms water (not acetate/O2) as the oxygen source.
